A.evidence: Faithful prose and Algorithm 1 with usable LaTeX; minor issues include repeated page headers/arXiv marginal text and section 3 downgraded/misnumbered as a top heading.

B.evidence: Severe LOC tokens, duplicated paragraphs, repeated Algorithm/section text, and wrong formulas such as beta powers and gradients make reading order and math largely broken.

C.evidence: Prose mostly present but author order is displaced, Algorithm 1 is a single dense code block, and many equations are replaced by formula-not-decoded markers.

D.evidence: Main prose is readable, but Algorithm 1 is truncated after m0 and theorem math is badly garbled; later section text is partially dropped.

E.evidence: Good prose and algorithm structure, but several formulas are wrong, e.g. beta2 correction becomes 1-beta2, alpha_t uses sqrt(T), and headings are inconsistently marked.

F.evidence: Early prose is readable, but page 3 collapses into massive tilde garbage and many later equations/theorem expressions are OCR gibberish.

G.evidence: Very faithful prose, algorithms, and displayed equations; minor issues include a literal \n in Algorithm 1, odd heading levels, and one rescaling formula typo.

H.evidence: Strong overall transcription, but starts with wrong arXiv version, has an incorrect alpha_t formula, hyphenation artifacts, and duplicated stray equations near Related Work.
